tax: Placed at order rank, Argillic Elementals is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a chemically or organically transformed earth body, not simple bulk rock or crystal. A new family belongs here only if it shows a distinct transformed-earth body-plan with its own chemistry and field signs.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a shimmering lime sheet, a hollow tufa tower, a pale crumbling figure, or a dark slumping mound. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a white lime film or clicking advance, or a gritty pale crumble versus a dark humic smear — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Argillic Elementals occupies lime-seep terraces, drip caverns, clay-limestone contacts, and organic-rich root channels. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where lime supersaturation deposits a calcite body, or earth-pressure animates a mixed-chemistry seam and recur with the seep, drip, or mixed seam replenishes the same nucleus, sustaining themselves on lime supersaturation in seep or drip, or earth-pressure in mixed chemistry.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members pose as natural flowstone, soil, or marl and rouse to chemical triggers — seep pressure, warmth, organic gradient — answering by jetting, spreading, entombing, or adhering. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a transformed-earth body, immunity to dilute acid (calcareous) or to piercing (organic), and a specific chemistry-failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: jetting, crust-spreading, entombing, and adhering. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Argillic Elementals matters because they make cavern floors, lime terraces, and root channels deceptively safe until trodden or tipped. It lets survey reports sort transformed-earth hazards by chemistry, so a crew knows whether to fear caustic jet, sealing crust, crumbling mass, or adhesion without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Argillic Elementals is the order-rank grouping of elementals whose bodies are chemically transformed or biogenically influenced earth — worked, mixed, or precipitated within the True Elementals. Its members share a body of earth that has passed through chemical or organic transformation: lime-precipitated, mixed clay-limestone, or organics-laden loam, and divide below into family groups by whether the chemistry is calcareous (chalk/tufa) or mixed organic-mineral (marl/loam). Argillic elementals are the earth class that most surprises Throat crews, because their bodies resemble soil and flowstone, not bare rock.
